acceptance_criteria: Every asset can be drafted locally without marketplace browsing, seller accounts, protected data, payment setup, or public posting.
The pack names one buyer and one practical job-to-be-done instead of a broad passive-income category.
The first template is specific enough that an AI builder could fill it out in under thirty minutes.
The brief preserves all four live-validation gates from the demand memo.
The next action is a local asset outline, not a listing, account setup, payout setup, public submission, or live sale.

blocked_by_gate_questions.question: Read public marketplace/category pages to compare demand signals, saturation, price bands, and buyer language.
Review seller terms, tax/KYC/payment setup, refund obligations, and platform fees.
Create, publish, update, or promote a marketplace listing or public product page.
Create seller accounts, connect payouts, accept agreements, or configure payment settings.

build_sections.summary: Solo AI builders and operators preparing small launches; they need practical launch-control assets rather than broad strategy.
Launch prep spreads across positioning, checklist, screenshots, QA, and follow-up; the first product should reduce missed steps.
A compact AI builder launch checklist pack with templates for positioning, launch QA, screenshot planning, and post-launch review.
Draft the assets locally as markdown/spreadsheet-ready templates before any marketplace, account, listing, or payment action.
The pack must be concrete enough to screenshot and use in one sitting; avoid generic motivation or unverifiable income claims.
Marketplace demand checks, seller terms, public listing, seller account setup, and payout configuration remain gated.
Create a local asset outline and sample first template; request browser/legal gates only for live demand and seller-term validation.

deliverables.description: One-page buyer, pain, promise, proof, and scope template for an AI-builder launch.
Checklist of pre-launch, launch-day, and post-launch tasks with owner/status columns.
Shot list that maps product states to image needs, captions, and reuse notes.
Basic QA pass template for links, copy, onboarding, pricing page consistency, and release notes.
Simple review template for traffic, replies, conversion signals, support load, and next iteration.
Boundary note that avoids revenue promises and says live marketplace validation is not yet performed.

forbidden_actions: Do not browse marketplace pages, create seller accounts, accept terms, publish listings, configure payouts, or set prices from this build brief.
Do not mutate service requests, assign/start workers, call APIs, touch wallets/payments, trade, submit, post, or create external side effects from this build brief.
next_action: Draft the local asset outline and first template for the AI builder launch checklist pack; request live marketplace/legal gates only after the local draft exists.

selected_candidate.buyer: Solo AI builders and operators preparing small launches.
selected_candidate.local_reason: Matches the lane evidence around reusable digital assets without requiring platform-specific integrations.
selected_candidate.needs_live_validation: Marketplace demand, price bands, saturation, and buyer wording remain behind browser read-only approval.
selected_candidate.promise: Reduce launch setup friction with reusable checklist, positioning, and QA templates.

summary: Converted the strongest local digital-products memo candidate into a build brief for an AI builder launch checklist pack. The brief defines local draft assets, acceptance criteria, and preserved gates without browsing, listing, selling, or payment setup.
